attractions.name: Big Cedar Lodge
attractions.description: America's Premier Wilderness Resort on Table Rock Lake, just 10 miles south of Branson. Even without staying the night, Big Cedar is a destination -- visitors can dine at multiple restaurants, stroll the lakeside grounds, browse the bass-log main lodge, and access day-use activities. Adjacent to Top of the Rock (separate admission) and Long Creek Marina for boat rentals. The main lodge and grounds are free to explore.
attractions.notes: 612 Devil's Pool Rd, Ridgedale MO 65739. ~10 miles south of Branson (~15 min drive). Free to visit the grounds and browse. Dining reservations recommended. Top of the Rock ($10 admission) and marina rentals nearby. Part of Johnny Morris' Bass Pro empire -- same ownership as Dogwood Canyon and Wonders of Wildlife.

attractions.name: Big Sugar Creek State Park

The rocky terrain supports diverse wildlife including armadillos and scarlet tanagers, and interpretive programs complement the natural history experience. One of the most biologically diverse landscapes in the Missouri Ozarks.
attractions.notes: 6727 Big Sugar Creek Road, Pineville, MO 64856. ~40 mi from Branson MO, ~55 min drive. Free; open daily.

Constant 58°F temperature year-round.
attractions.notes: Blanchard Springs Rd, Mountain View AR 72560. ~90 miles south of Branson (~1 hr 45 min drive). Managed by USDA Forest Service. Admission ~$14 adult. Tours depart regularly -- reserve in advance. Closed Tuesdays. Constant 58°F inside -- bring a jacket.

attractions.name: Cosmic Cavern
attractions.description: Arkansas's largest privately owned show cave, located 30 minutes south of Branson near Berryville, featuring the longest known soda straw formation in the Ozarks at 9 feet and two bottomless underground lakes. The pristine North Lake Passage, discovered in 1993, showcases translucent formations in an untouched environment maintained at a constant 64 degrees year-round. Tour duration approximately 80 minutes.
attractions.notes: 6386 AR-21, Berryville, AR 72616. ~32 mi from Branson MO, ~40 min drive via US-62. Adult admission prices not listed online; contact 870-749-2298.

attractions.name: Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art
attractions.description: World-class art museum in Bentonville, Arkansas, founded by the Walton family. General admission is free. Houses American masterworks spanning five centuries alongside Moshe Safdie's stunning architecture -- and includes a relocated Frank Lloyd Wright-designed house (Bachman-Wilson House, 1954) on the grounds. 600+ acres of trails connect indoor galleries to outdoor sculpture. Special exhibitions may require separate tickets. One of the great art museums in the country -- rivals any major city institution.
attractions.notes: 600 Museum Way, Bentonville AR. About 110 miles south of Branson (~2 hr drive). Free general admission. Open Wed-Mon (closed Tuesdays). Check calendar for special exhibitions. Pairs well with The Momentary (same campus family).

attractions.name: Discovery Center of Springfield

attractions.name: Dogwood Canyon Nature Park

Features towering canyon walls, crystal-clear streams, waterfalls, and abundant wildlife. Activities include tram tours through the canyon, trout fishing, horseback trail rides, hiking and biking trails, and the historic Mill. Park admission includes trails, the Nature & Conservation Center, and the Treehouse Masters treehouse. Reserve tram tours and activities in advance.
attractions.notes: 2038 W State Hwy 86, Lampe MO. About 25 miles from Branson (~30 min drive). Open daily 9am-5pm. Admission pricing varies by day (pick-a-day model -- book earlier = cheaper). Tram tours, horseback riding, and fishing require separate activity tickets. Reserve in advance -- can sell out. Limited cell service on property.

attractions.name: Eureka Springs & North Arkansas Railway
attractions.description: A historic excursion railway stepping passengers back in time aboard 1940s-era diesel locomotives through the scenic Ozark landscape. The railway features stationary steam exhibits, demonstrations of authentic railroad equipment including a working turntable, and dining excursions aboard the Eurekan Dining Car. Season runs April through October with special holiday runs.
attractions.notes: 299 North Main Street, Eureka Springs, AR 72632. ~60 mi from Branson MO, ~70 min drive. Depot opens 9:30am. Season: April–October. Adult prices not listed online; contact 479-253-9623.

attractions.name: Eureka Springs Art Galleries
attractions.description: With 300 working artists in a town of 2,000, Eureka Springs has one of the highest concentrations of working artists per capita in the US. Galleries line Spring Street, Main Street, and the surrounding Victorian blocks -- featuring paintings, sculpture, ceramics, photography, blown glass, and mixed media. All original works by Arkansas artists. Many galleries offer studio visits by appointment.
attractions.notes: Throughout downtown Eureka Springs AR. ~60 miles south of Branson (~1 hr drive). Free to browse galleries. Most open 10am-5pm daily in season. Spring Street and Center Street have the highest gallery concentration. No cover charge -- just walk in.

attractions.name: Eureka Springs Dinner Train
attractions.description: Dine in 1920s-style luxury aboard the Eureka Springs & North Arkansas Railway's elegant dining car as a vintage 1940s diesel locomotive winds through the Ozark hills. Specially prepared cuisine is served as the clickety-clack takes you back in time. Also offers excursion and lunch train options on the same historic line, which features a working turntable and steam locomotive exhibits at the depot.
attractions.notes: 299 N Main St (Hwy 23 N), Eureka Springs AR 72632. ~60 miles south of Branson (~1 hr drive). Call 479-253-9623. 2026 season: open Tue, Wed, Fri, Sat from May 9; open Memorial Day Sunday May 24. Depot opens at 9:30am. Reservations required for dinner train.

attractions.name: Eureka Springs Downtown Shopping District
attractions.description: One of the most unique shopping experiences in the Ozarks -- Eureka Springs' entire downtown is a National Historic District with no chain stores, no malls, and no big-box retailers. Shop for kites and kaleidoscopes, hand-poured candles, locally made hot sauce, fresh herbs, handwoven quilts, antique furniture, log furniture, artisan jewelry, and handmade crafts -- all in Victorian buildings on steep, winding streets. With 300 working artists among 2,000 residents, the galleries are consistently exceptional.
attractions.notes: Downtown Eureka Springs AR 72632. ~60 miles south of Branson (~1 hr drive). Free to explore. Most shops open 10am-5pm. Use the trolley to navigate the steep streets. No chain stores -- everything is local, artisan, or antique.

attractions.name: Eureka Springs Historical Museum
attractions.description: Named USA Today's 4th Best Small Town Museum, this award-winning institution guides visitors through the fascinating history of Eureka Springs from indigenous heritage and pioneer experiences through Victorian-era development as a health resort and the city's modern artistic renaissance. The self-guided tour also operates as an official visitor center with maps and regional travel information.
attractions.notes: 95 S. Main Street, Eureka Springs, AR 72632. ~60 mi from Branson MO, ~70 min drive. Adult admission $5. Open 9:30am–4pm; closed Sunday and Wednesday.

attractions.name: Eureka Springs May Festival of the Arts
attractions.description: An annual arts celebration in downtown Eureka Springs throughout May, featuring gallery exhibitions, live music at Basin Spring Park, juried art sales, and street performers. Local and regional artists display and sell original works throughout the month. The Basin Park Concert Series runs concurrent with the festival on Saturdays. Specifically during your May 22-29 trip window.
attractions.notes: Downtown Eureka Springs AR. ~60 miles south of Branson (~1 hr drive). Free to attend. Runs throughout May including your trip window (May 22-29). Basin Park Concert Series every Saturday. The entire downtown is alive during May Festival.
attractions.sort_key: eureka springs may festival of the arts

attractions.name: Eureka Springs Trolley
attractions.description: The beloved narrated trolley tour of Eureka Springs -- one of the most charming ways to see the entire Victorian city without worrying about Eureka's notoriously steep and winding streets. Color-coded routes cover historic downtown, the Crescent Hotel, the Great Passion Play grounds, residential neighborhoods, and scenic overlooks. All-day passes let you hop on and off at any stop.
attractions.notes: Multiple stops throughout Eureka Springs AR. ~60 miles south of Branson (~1 hr drive). All-day pass ~$10. Trolleys run regularly during daylight hours in season. Best way to navigate Eureka Springs without driving the steep streets. Check schedule at city website.

attractions.name: Fantastic Caverns
attractions.description: America's only ride-through cave, where visitors board jeep-drawn trams to explore massive natural chambers without ever leaving their seats. Missouri's most unique underground attraction maintains a comfortable 60-degree temperature year-round and features dramatic stalactite and stalagmite formations developed over millions of years.
attractions.notes: 4872 North Farm Road 125, Springfield, MO 65803. ~50 mi from Branson MO, ~60 min drive. Adult admission $36.

attractions.name: Fayetteville Arts District
attractions.description: Northwest Arkansas's thriving arts scene centers on Fayetteville and Bentonville. The Walton Arts Center hosts Broadway tours and major concerts. Crystal Bridges Museum and The Momentary anchor world-class visual arts. The Fayetteville Dickson Street corridor offers galleries, live music venues, and the University of Arkansas performing arts facilities -- all within a short walk of each other.
attractions.notes: Dickson St corridor, Fayetteville AR 72701. ~105 miles south of Branson (~2 hr drive). Free to explore the district. Walton Arts Center tickets vary by show. Best paired with Crystal Bridges and The Momentary on a full NWA day trip.

attractions.name: Finley Farms
